name: Tozal Solitario from Albalatillo — circular through the Monegros
name_original: Tozal Solitario desde Albalatillo — circular por los Monegros
region_intro: Hiking around Albalatillo offers routes through the unique Monegros landscape, characterized by semi-arid terrain, low hills, and occasional lagoons. The region's topography includes gentle elevations, such as the Tozal Solitario and Tozal de la Cobeta, providing varied perspectives. Trails often traverse open country, with some routes circling natural water bodies like the Laguna de Sariñena. The area provides a mix of flat sections and gradual ascents, suitable for different hiking preferences. Best hiking trails around Albalatillo The most popular hiking route is Tozal Solitario from Albalatillo — circular through the Monegros, a 15.5 miles (24.98 km) trail that takes 6 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers expansive views across the Monegros region. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna de Sariñena Loop, an easy 4.7 miles (7.56 km) path. This trail circles the Laguna de Sariñena, providing views of the wetland environment. Local hikers also love the Tozal de la Cobeta loop from Ermita de San Miguel, a 3.9 miles (6.33 km) trail leading through the landscape around Tozal de la Cobeta, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Hiking around Albalatillo is defined by the Monegros landscape, lagoon areas, and low hills, offering a range of experiences from easy walks to more challenging routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including family friendly hiking trails Albalatillo. The routes in Albalatillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Albalatillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Albalatillo?
A: There are 6 hiking routes documented on komoot for the Albalatillo area, offering a range of experiences from easy walks to more challenging treks. These include 4 easy, 1 moderate, and 1 difficult route.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families in Albalatillo?
A: Yes, Albalatillo offers several easy trails perfect for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947118675/laguna-de-sarinena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Sariñena Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.7-mile (7.56 km) path that circles the Laguna de Sariñena, providing views of the wetland environment. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta loop from Ermita de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.9-mile (6.33 km) trail.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in the Albalatillo area?
A: Many of the trails around Albalatillo are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1091909324/tozal-solitario-from-albalatillo-circular-through-the-monegros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal Solitario from Albalatillo — circular through the Monegros&lt;/a&gt;, the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947118675/laguna-de-sarinena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Sariñena Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta loop from La Clamor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect on hikes around Albalatillo?
A: Hiking around Albalatillo takes you through the unique Monegros landscape, characterized by semi-arid terrain, low hills, and occasional lagoons. You'll find a mix of open country, gentle elevations like the Tozal Solitario and Tozal de la Cobeta, and routes circling natural water bodies, offering varied perspectives.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or places to explore near Albalatillo's hiking trails?
A: The region offers several points of interest. You can explore natural formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozales de Monegros&lt;/a&gt;. For a historical and cultural experience, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charterhouse of Our Lady of Las Fuentes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Albalatillo?
A: The trails in Albalatillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique Monegros landscape, the tranquility of the routes, and the opportunity to experience the region's natural beauty, including the lagoons and low hills.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Monegros region?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1091909324/tozal-solitario-from-albalatillo-circular-through-the-monegros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal Solitario from Albalatillo — circular through the Monegros&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15.5-mile (24.98 km) trail specifically known for offering expansive views across the Monegros region.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Albalatillo?
A: The Monegros region, including Albalatillo, experiences hot summers. Therefore, the best seasons for hiking are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities. Winter can also be pleasant, though mornings may be chilly.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water bodies or wetlands?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947118675/laguna-de-sarinena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Sariñena Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This easy trail circles the Laguna de Sariñena, providing direct views and an experience of the wetland environment, which is a significant feature of the Monegros landscape.

Q: What should I pack for a summer hike in Albalatillo?
A: Given the semi-arid climate of the Monegros region, summer hikes in Albalatillo require careful preparation. Essential items include plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunglasses, high-SPF sunscreen), lightweight and breathable clothing, and sturdy hiking shoes. Starting early in the morning to avoid the midday heat is also advisable.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty trails for a slightly longer hike?
A: For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta loop from La Clamor&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 5.3 miles (8.6 km) long and typically takes around 2 hours 18 minutes to complete, offering a good balance of distance and elevation.
tips:
Cycling or walking here is like crossing the heart of the Monegros landscape: arid terrain, ochre colors, and that solitary atmosphere that makes this region so special.
On windy days (like during the Orbea Monegros 2025), this area becomes even more epic: the dust, the air, and the contours of the terrain make you feel like you're on another planet.
Nice hike between the washed out bizarre mountains.
